preamble: The lenition of a voiced alveolar stop /d/ into a rhotic consonant (typically a flap [ɾ]), a common process in intervocalic position.

examples.note: Intervocalic /d/ becomes an alveolar flap [ɾ] between vowels (tap rule).
Retroflex stop becoming a retroflex flap [ɽ] in intervocalic position.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages. Blackwell.
